title: Opinions of the Akagi women
wikitext:
[]: Opinions of the Akagi women             Quote Post by soda7777777 » Tue Aug 03, 2010 5:33 pm Maybe it's just me, but I feel both Ritsuko and her mother are relatively unstable. Just the fact that they were incredibly jealous of Rei goes to show it. They both cause damage to Gendo as well, thinking he no longer wants them. So I was wondering, what everyone else's opinion of them is? Personally, I never cared for them much. But that's just me.

[]: Re: Opinions of the Akagi women             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 03, 2010 5:55 pm The two of them are quite neurotic, for sure. Naoko, what little we see of her, did not strike me as terribly likeable; Ritsuko, OTOH, came over as my kind of woman.

[]: Re: Opinions of the Akagi women             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Aug 03, 2010 6:02 pm soda7777777 wrote: Maybe it's just me, but I feel all the characters are relatively unstable.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Tue Aug 03, 2010 6:37 pm I'll never like the mother after seeing her choke Rei, she was so beautiful and you just killed her so quickly! Dam You!!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Tue Aug 03, 2010 6:57 pm They are both fools. You know therapists would tell you that it's because they are so smart that they are so stupid. Intelligent people who are driven more by reason than emotions and consider themselves intellectual are often more subsebtible to suduction than people that more emotionally driven.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Aug 03, 2010 7:09 pm Naoko deserved a harsher death. Ritsuko just needs some help. Instrumentality probably did wonders for her, so she's ok.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Tue Aug 03, 2010 8:09 pm I didn’t really like either of them. I felt they were both to smart to let themselves be ruled by their emotions.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Tue Aug 03, 2010 8:48 pm Under the right circumstances, most can be ruled by emotion. But perhaps their specialization was their undoing. Super-science is a search for answers, while the heart has only questions. This I say unto thee. Seriously, remember that anyone working on Project Apocalypsetown was probably so devoted to their craft that they ignored everything else. Gifted students are disproportionately loopy, compared to their peers, and there are plenty of theories as to why. One is that they're so oversensitive to some things that they ignore all others, and therefore have poor social skills. Another is that mental illness and genius/creativity may be linked in some way. Another is demonic possession.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 03, 2010 9:42 pm I always thought there was some delicious parallelism between them. If only Gendo had killed Naoko it would have been more complete.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Aug 04, 2010 2:58 am I never found my self to involved with Naoko, but i think Ritsuko as a character is great. One of the things that struck me about her, as soon as we learned about her backstory, is that she tries to distance herself from her mother (bleaching her hair), trying to step out of her shadow, to become more than "Dr. Akagi's daughter", yet ultimately goes down on almost exactly the same path. Deliciously ironic.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 04, 2010 9:29 am I think it's hard for anyone to get into Naoko Ikagi's character, seeing as how she's in the series for only part of one flash-back episode. To get all of her character's intricacies you need to look at NERV's MAGI system, as they are programed as the different parts of Naoko. Especially CASPAR since it had Naoko's actual brain in it, which ultimately betrayed Rituko in EoE. Another thing about that whole mother daughter relationship between the two after NERV's launch is that it's grossly similar to how the Evangelion operate in the series. Each contain the spirit, or in Rituko's case, the mind, of the of the operator's mother. And in each case the mother seems to play a large role in the protection or betrayal of the children.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Aug 04, 2010 9:44 am i found them both very believable, and i always got the feeling that their motivations were easier to understand than many of the other characters'... i just never really liked either of them. don't hate them, but never cared for them either way. i was more on the "oh, suck it up and quit the hystrionics" camp when it came to both of them, to be honest. (of course, it's entirely possible that i just thought i understood their motivations but really didn't. IDEK).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 04, 2010 3:58 pm Naoko, I was never really fond of with what little will saw of her, though it's somewhat understandable that she snapped given her jealously of Yui/Rei 1. If she would have been fleshed out more, I probably would have had more sympathy for her, although I do like the "overworked parent that's never really there for their child" vibe we get from her, somewhat similar to implications with Gendo/Doctor Katsuragi/whoever else. Ritsuko... I understand her being in love with Gendo, yet I never fully understood her motivations (other than her being jealous of Rei/Yui/her own mother), so in that sense, I find it hard to understand her, let alone relate to her. Put it this way, I have an easier time understanding Gendo than Ritsuko, though both (all?) characters are misunderstood, anyway. FWIW, I don't dislike Ritsuko or any of the characters, but I do think Seele are a bunch of batshit insane fucks, considering they ARE with that death cult (see EoE when pre-3rd Impact wackiness chanting starts occurring) of theirs. It doesn't help we never get inside Seele's collective (har har) heads with the exception of supplemental/whatever materials more or less saying they think they're doing the right thing for the world because humanity's at a(n evolutionary) dead end, blah blah blah.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Aug 04, 2010 10:08 pm Naoko's instability always struck me as a little forced, mostly because we don't get nearly enough time for her - Anno tries to really quickly establish her personality through a couple of scenes and her letters to Ritsuko, and it sort of makes clear that she's not a very good mother and is also a bit of a bitch, but it doesn't do nearly enough to make the depths of her (never properly explained) obsession with Gendou obvious, so it feels a little wtf when she just up and kills Rei. Unless you want to say that Rei with a complete, non-divided Lilith soul is just innately creepy and inspires deep-seated crazy to show itself in otherwise normal people. Which, to be sure, fits rather well with Lilith's general behavior. I still love Ritsuko, though. I'd say that her character being removed is one of Rebuild's greatest crimes, if not for the fact that every other character I loved has gotten pretty much the same deal.

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Wed Aug 04, 2010 10:35 pm I love them both because they're mentally unstable, logic whores who get fucked over by the same guy. I didn't really see Naoko as anything else than a means to develop Ritsuko more fully and what we see of her is more than enough to do that. Ritsuko's struggle, and failure to, comes to terms with herself as being different from her mother is just delicious and it's part of what makes the last few episodes so heart-wrenching. Parallelism and irony are the spices that pepper the steak of their tragedy.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 04, 2010 11:08 pm Azathoth wrote: Unless you want to say that Rei with a complete, non-divided Lilith soul is just innately creepy and inspires deep-seated crazy to show itself in otherwise normal people. Which, to be sure, fits rather well with Lilith's general behavior.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Thu Aug 05, 2010 2:22 pm I never found my self to involved with Naoko, but i think Ritsuko as a character is great.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Aug 05, 2010 9:13 pm I honestly don't understand either of them. Naoko evidently had a crush on Gendo, and was jealous of Yui. That part, I get. What I don't get is why she suddenly wants to sleep with Gendo when she became head of Project E. I also don't get why she was willing to choke Rei as opposed to Gendo when Rei confessed the truth of their relationship. Ritsuko makes even less sense. Yeah yeah, I get the irony bit of her falling for the same trap as her mother, but that what wrecks it. She's supposed to be a genius, and yet somehow she falls head over heels for the guy who's indirectly responsible for her mother's death. Logic would dictate that Ritsuko would hate Gendo's guts over this.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Aug 06, 2010 12:58 am CyberXIII wrote: Logic would dictate that Ritsuko would hate Gendo's guts over this.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 06, 2010 1:03 am CyberXIII wrote: Logic would dictate that Ritsuko would hate Gendo's guts over this.
